channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Biden eulogizes Ethel Kennedy as 'hero' who put her own stamp on country
item.description: President Joe Biden has capped a memorial service for Ethel Kennedy by recalling the wife of the late Sen. Robert F.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking And Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Man accused of threatening FEMA workers in North Carolina called on others 'to join'
item.description: The man says he believed workers were withholding supplies, but soon discovered that wasn't the case.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, WGN 9

item.title: Ex-Las Vegas-area Democratic politician gets at least 28 years in prison for killing reporter
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A former Las Vegas-area Democratic elected official was sentenced Wednesday to serve at least 28 years in Nevada state prison for killing an investigative journalist who wrote articles critical of his conduct in office two years ago and exposed an intimate relationship with a female
item.dc:creator: KEN RITTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota school district bans officer from teaching after he reenacted George Floyd's murder
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A Minnesota school district has banned a police officer from working as a substitute teacher after a series of “racially harmful” actions that officials say included putting a student on the ground for a reenactment of the 2020 murder of George Floyd by a Minneapolis officer.
item.dc:creator: BY STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jimmy Carter, at age 100, casts his 2024 ballot by mail
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — Jimmy Carter cast his ballot in the 2024 election Wednesday. The former president voted by mail, the Carter Center confirmed in a statement. It happened barely two weeks after Carter celebrated his 100th birthday on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Clint Eastwood's daughter arrested in Beverly Hills in domestic violence case
item.description: "Based on statements and injuries,” the 31-year-old Los Angeles resident was arrested and booked at the BHPD Jail on suspicion of felony domestic violence.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about shaken baby syndrome as a Texas man could be first in US executed over it
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A Texas man this week could become the first person executed in the U.S. for a murder conviction tied to the diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Navy fighter jet with two on board crashes during training in Washington state
item.description: The U.S. Navy searched Wednesday through mountainous terrain for two crew members who were aboard a fighter jet that crashed in Washington state during a routine training flight. The EA-18G Growler jet from the Electronic Attack Squadron crashed east of Mount Rainier at about 3:23 p.m.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man shot in Las Vegas casino pleads guilty to stabbing 2 hotel employees
item.description: The man who stabbed two Las Vegas resort employees before a security officer shot him in the middle of the casino entered a plea Tuesday and will be sentenced in November, records said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: After 36 years, accused killer in Oregon cold case to stand trial
item.description: An autopsy revealed Deborah Atrops was physically assaulted and strangled to death. She was 30. Then, the case went cold.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, KRON4

item.title: Trump: Black and Hispanic voters who vote for Harris 'gotta have your head examined'
item.description: “Any African American or Hispanic– and you know how well I’m doing there – that votes for Kamala, you gotta have your head examined,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Colorado video shows Tren de Aragua gang beating apartment complex worker in extortion bid, company says
item.description: CBZ Management in Colorado claims local government and police are overlooking gang activity at their apartment complexes and shared video of an attack on an employee.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ISIS-K orchestrated foiled Election Day terror plot: US official
item.description: Afghan native Nasir Ahmad Tawhedi, 27, was arrested in early October and accused of planning to target people on Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Robert Telles sentenced for murder of Las Vegas journalist Jeff German
item.description: Robert Telles is accused of plotting to kill Las Vegas Review-Journal reporter Jeff German, 69, after he wrote some unflattering news stories about him that he felt caused him to lose the election.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, NewsNation

item.title: Biden delivers final eulogy during Ethel Kennedy memorial service in DC
item.description: Ethel Kennedy, 96, died last week. She had been hospitalized after suffering a stroke earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Charges filed against Utah woman accused of murdering, burying husband
item.description: Gledhill allegedly put Johnson's body in a rooftop storage container and slid it down the stairs and into the back of their minivan, the informant said.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, NewsNation

item.title: Clint Eastwood's daughter arrested in Beverly Hills in domestic violence case
item.description: Actor Francesca Eastwood, daughter of legendary actor Clint Eastwood, was arrested in Beverly Hills over the weekend on suspicion of felony domestic violence, according to reporting from the Los Angeles Times.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, ABC 4

item.title: Navy: Fighter jet with two on board crashed in Washington state during training
item.description: The U.S. Navy was searching for two aviators who went missing after their plane crashed during a routine training flight in Washington state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: FEMA workers threatened by armed group in Tennessee
item.description: "They were all armed, open carry not guns drawn, but they had surrounded them and there was a lady there that was yelling and threatening them.
item.dc:creator: Karen Jenkins, ABC 4

item.title: Iowa emerges as key battleground in race for the House
item.description: In their fight to flip the House, Democrats are eyeing gains in what may seem an unlikely spot: Iowa. Two GOP seats in the Hawkeye State have emerged as true battlegrounds in the final stretch of the campaign, as some election forecasters have moved Reps.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon, Google make dueling nuclear investments to power data centers with clean energy
item.description: Amazon on Wednesday said that it was investing in small nuclear reactors, coming just two days after a similar announcement by Google, as both tech giants seek new sources of carbon-free electricity to meet surging demand from data centers and artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Ozzy Osbourne guitarist Jake E. Lee shot while walking dog in Las Vegas
item.description: Guitarist Jake E. Lee, who formerly played for Ozzy Osbourne, was shot Tuesday while walking his dog, his publicist said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, Fox 5

item.title: Voters in California and Nevada consider ban on forced labor aimed at protecting prisoners
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California and Nevada voters will decide in November whether to ban forced prison labor by removing language from their state constitutions rooted in the legacy of chattel slavery.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN and RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why are scientists so interested in Europa?
item.description: NASA's Europa Clipper soared into space Monday, marking a major step toward exploring one of the most intriguing moons in our solar system.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: North Carolina football player dies from rare lung cancer at 23
item.description: UNC had honored Tylee Craft, 23, during Saturday's game. Coach Mack Brown, however, said he hadn't learned of Craft's death until after the game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Armed man arrested near Trump rally denies 'assassination attempt' claims
item.description: “I’m an artist, I’m the last person that would cause any violence and harm to anybody,” the man has since said.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump suggests using National Guard, military against 'enemy from within'
item.description: Former President Donald Trump in a Sunday interview suggested using the National Guard or the military on Election Day to combat what he described as potential chaos from “the enemy from within” – a group that Trump said includes “radical left lunatics.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Senate rankings: 5 seats most likely to flip
item.description: The battle for the Senate majority has hit its final month as Republicans race to end their four-year stint in the minority. The GOP needs to win only one of two competitive races in red states — Montana and Ohio — to do just that, barring a surprise loss elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Middle East latest: US says it will send THAAD defense battery to Israel, along with US troops
item.description: The U.S. is sending a Terminal High Altitude Area Defense battery to Israel, along with US troops to operate it. The Pentagon made the announcement just hours after Iran warned the U.S. to keep its military forces out of Israel.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump calls for adding 10,000 Border Patrol agents after derailing a bipartisan border bill
item.description: PRESCOTT VALLEY, Ariz. (AP) — Former President Donald Trump on Sunday proposed hiring 10,000 additional Border Patrol agents and giving them a $10,000 retention and signing bonus, after he derailed a bipartisan bill earlier this year that included funding for more border personnel.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What $35 trillion in US national debt means for Americans
item.description: With the national debt standing at more than $35 trillion, economists fear that the country's debt load will only grow.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, Fox 5

item.title: Euthanasia, pronouns, sports betting: Intriguing 2024 ballot measures
item.description: Several states have placed important measures on their ballots, ranging from flag designs to end-of-life care.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, ABC 4

item.title: From the pulpit, Harris calls out Trump for hurricane misinformation. Biden surveys Florida's damage
item.description: GREENVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Kamala Harris used an appearance Sunday before a largely Black church audience in battleground North Carolina to call out Donald Trump for spreading misinformation about the government's hurricane response.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, AAMER MADHANI and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Sam's Club opening location in Texas, but there's no checkout lanes
item.description: Sam's Club CEO Chris Nicholas says the warehouse club chain will soon open a store in Texas but there won't be any checkout lanes.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, Fox 5

item.title: Nearly 200 miles of Blue Ridge Parkway reopens as long-term work remains in North Carolina
item.description: The Blue Ridge Parkway was closed indefinitely after Hurricane Helene swept through the region last month.
item.dc:creator: QCN Digital Staff, WGN 9

item.title: Trump calls for protester to 'go back home to Mommy' during California rally
item.description: Trump called for the protester to “get the hell knocked out of her,” his latest instance of using violent language when confronted by demonstrators.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: AP Top 25: Oregon, Penn State move behind No. 1 Texas. Army, Navy both ranked for 1st time since '60
item.description: Oregon and Penn State each moved up a spot in The Associated Press college football poll on Sunday following thrilling wins in high-profile games, and Top 25 newcomers Navy and Army are in the rankings together for the first time since 1960.
item.dc:creator: ERIC OLSON, Associated Press, Fox 5
